title: some subfolders not visible in thunderbird 140.10
creator: dwain0
content:
I am using Thunderbird on a Macbook Pro 13-inch mid-2012, in a POP3 account

Thunderbird 140.10 is generally running fine, except that some sub-folders are not visible in my Thunderbird email tool view. I have a hierarchical

I have tried repairing the parent folders, but that had no effect.

The corresponding .sbd files are clearly visible in ~/<user>/Library>/<profile>/Mail/Local Folders/pop-server.<account> and being backed up in Time Machine backups.

Curiously, there is a much-truncated replica of some .sbd folders in Local Folders named /<folder-name>.sbd, all dated in 2013, yet the folder was created in 2017. I presume these refer to an update of Thunderbird that left some trash, and of no consequence. These are parallel to Archives and Archives.msf files.

Please advise on how to disambiguate this structure and recover views of existing mail folders not being displayed.

title: Thunderbird Calendar Sync issues with Apple Calendar using version 150.0 (64 bit)
creator: wongi
content:
Dear Community,

I have a LAN connected desktop Windows 11 computer using Thunderbird 140.10.0esr (32-bit) synced to two different Apple calendars with no issues.

I also have two Wi-Fi connected laptop Windows 11 computers using Thunderbird 150.0 (64-bit) each synced to the same two Apple calendars above. I haven't had any sync issues with these until lately and I believe they are setup exactly the same as the desktop computer above.

For example if I attempt to "Add" a calendar entry I will get the following error message: "If you're seeing this message after snoozing or dismissing a reminder and this is for a calendar you do not want to add edit events for, you can mark this calendar as read-only to avoid such experience in future. To do so, get to the calendar properties by right clicking on this calendar in the list in the calendar or task view."

I have tried removing the calendar and adding it back but I still get the same intermittent sync issues and get these type of error messages. I have no extensions.

Not sure if this might be a software bug in the latest 150.0 (64-bit) release because 140.10esr (32-bit) appears to work fine on my desktop computer.

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Many thanks.

title: Thunderbird keeps asking me for password to an account that doesn't exist at my server.
creator: cascade3
content:
When I launch T-bird on my laptop, it shows "Enter your password for UCIm2Lqoc@gwtc.net on imap.gwtc.net:" There is no such email address. It is one that I briefly attempted to set up, then decided against it and used a different one. I have just now confirmed by telephone with my provider's helpdesk that there is no account with that name on their server. 

    By simply hitting enter, that request disappears and T-bird shows me the messages for my primary email address, which works properly.    But when I try to access my second email address, I keep getting the same request.

First it says: "Login to account "cindyreed@gwtc.net" failed." and instructs me to "Enter your password for UCIm2Lqoc@gwtc.net on imap.gwtc.net:" And then, in response to everything I have tried so far: " Login to server imap.gwtc.net with username UCIm2Lqoc@gwtc.net failed." I would like to find out how to get out of this trap. Fortunately, I am able to access this second address on my cell-phone, but that is not much help when I am so frequently out of service area. Thank you. Plains Gardener

title: Nachrichten konnten nicht in die Mailbox-Datei geschrieben werden.
creator: robinhaas
content:
Hi. I´m running thunderbird 150.0 under win10 an a HP2760p with 8 GB RAM and 155 GB of free SDD-space.

I´ve got the errormessage: "Nachrichten konnten nicht in die Mailbox-Datei geschrieben werden. Stellen Sie sicher, dass das Dateisystem Ihnen Schreibrechte einräumt und genügend Speicherplatz zur Verfügung steht, um die Mailbox zu kopieren."

That is: Unable to write mesages to the mailbox-file. Make shure to have writing rights according to the filesystem and enaugh space is available to copy the mailbox.

I can´t find a limitation to filesize. I deleted a lot of mails, the error persists. I updated from thunderbird 140 to 150.0. The error persists. I downloaded my emails on an win11 Laptop. The download stopped after a while with the same error.

What can I do ? Thanks in advance - Robin

title: Wrong sender's name
creator: john391
content:
A few months ago I installed Thunderbird onto a new PC having previously used it on a previous PC (so I'm not a newbie).. However I'm seeing a persistent issue that I never saw before...

I've a regular contact whose name I won't specify but let's call him Roger Smith. What happens is that whenever I post something on a forum (such as this one) and somebody replies, the replies look as if they came from Roger Smith - even though he's got no connection with the actual forum.

It doesn't happen with every forum - for example it doesn't happen with this one - but it does affect most of them. Has anyone else ever reported this and is there anything I can do to correct it?
